attractions.name: Ale Asylum
attractions.description: Ale Asylum is a prominent Madison-based craft brewery known for its bold, unfiltered beers and "unapologetically insane" spirit. After a temporary closure, the brand returned to its roots and is now brewing its iconic lineup, including the flagship Hopalicious pale ale, at the Karben4 facility on Madison's east side. Ale Asylum, Journal Sentinel

attractions.name: American Players Theatre
attractions.description: American Players Theatre (APT) is a professional, classic theater located on 110 acres of hilly woods and meadows. It features two performance spaces: the 1,075-seat outdoor Hill Theatre amphitheater and the 201-seat indoor Touchstone Theatre. From June through November, APT produces nine plays in rotating repertory, ranking as the country's second largest outdoor theater devoted to the classics. About APT
attractions.address: 5950 Golf Course Road, Spring Green, WI 53588
attractions.days_open: Daily from June through October (Box Office open Monday-Sunday); reduced hours March-May and October-November. Box Office Hours

Touchstone Theatre: A more intimate 201-seat indoor performance space. About APT
Rotating Repertory: Nine plays produced annually from June through November. About APT

Driftless Area Location: Situated in the scenic rolling hills of southwest Wisconsin. Area Guide
Educational Programs: Includes a student matinee series reaching over 7,000 students annually. Student Matinee Series

attractions.name: Ancora Cafe + Bakery
attractions.description: Ancora Cafe + Bakery is a long-standing Madison staple that has evolved from a cozy downtown coffee shop into a four-location family of vibrant cafes. Known for its artfully crafted brunch dishes and expertly pulled espresso, it offers a welcoming, inclusive space for the community to gather over creative meals and house-made bakery items. (Ancora Cafes)

Expertly roasted organic and fair trade coffees with 15+ varieties available for purchase (Ancora Cafes)

Inclusive and communal atmosphere with both indoor and outdoor seating (Ancora Cafes)
Locally owned and operated since 1994 (Ancora Cafes)

attractions.name: Anthology
attractions.description: Anthology is a charming paper and craft shop in downtown Madison, owned by sisters Laura and Sachi Komai, that facilitates creativity by selling works from over 100 local and independent artists. The store offers a unique selection of Madison-themed gifts, art supplies, and handmade goods, while also providing a space for community members to create and engage with the local arts scene.
highlights: Owned and operated by local sisters Laura and Sachi Komai since 2008.
Features a curated selection of works from over 100 local and Midwest indie artists.
Specializes in Madison and Wisconsin-themed merchandise, including posters, stickers, and tea towels.

attractions.name: Atwood Avenue (Schenk-Atwood Neighborhood)
attractions.description: Atwood Avenue serves as the vibrant commercial corridor of the Schenk-Atwood-Starkweather-Yahara (SASY) neighborhood on Madison's near-east side. Known for its funky, bohemian vibe, the area features an eclectic mix of locally owned shops, ethnic restaurants, and historic landmarks like the Barrymore Theatre. Formerly a working-class industrial district, it has evolved into a highly walkable and bike-friendly community nestled between Lake Monona and the Yahara River (The 608 Team, Texas Lifestyle Magazine).
attractions.address: 2000-3400 blocks of Atwood Avenue, Madison, WI 53704
highlights: Barrymore Theatre: A historic 1929 venue hosting live music, comedy, and community events (Barrymore Theatre).
Olbrich Botanical Gardens: 16 acres of stunning outdoor gardens and a tropical conservatory located at the eastern end of the avenue (Olbrich Botanical Gardens).
Garver Feed Mill: A restored historic factory building reimagined as a community hub for local food producers, events, and wellness (Garver Feed Mill).
AtwoodFest: An annual neighborhood music festival held every July that draws thousands to the street for live performances (AtwoodFest).
Gail Ambrosius Chocolatier: A nationally recognized shop specializing in hand-crafted dark chocolate truffles (OnMilwaukee).
Goodman Community Center: A vital social hub housed in a repurposed ironworks building (SASY Neighborhood Association).

attractions.name: Babcock Hall Dairy Store
attractions.description: Babcock Hall Dairy Store is a historic university dairy facility and ice cream parlor located on the University of Wisconsin-Madison campus. Established in 1951, it serves as a fully functional dairy plant that produces award-winning ice cream and cheese while training future food industry professionals. Visitors can enjoy a wide variety of signature dairy products, sandwiches, and a complimentary observation experience of the production process.
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday

Historical significance as the nation's oldest university dairy facility

attractions.name: Badger Prairie County Park
attractions.description: Badger Prairie County Park is a 317-acre natural area serving as the center of the Ice Age Trail Junction Area. It features rolling prairie terrain, extensive restoration areas for grassland birds, and serves as a gateway to major regional trail systems. Dane County Parks, Trailforks

Originally opened in 1929 as the Eastwood Theater, it has evolved into a popular independent landmark hosting concerts, comedy, film, and community events. Official Website

attractions.name: Bascom Hill (Bascom Hill Historic District)
attractions.description: Bascom Hill is the iconic main quadrangle and historic core of the University of Wisconsin–Madison campus, forming a steep glacial drumlin that offers sweeping views of the Wisconsin State Capitol. Crowned by the majestic Bascom Hall and anchored by a famous statue of Abraham Lincoln, it serves as a central gathering place for students and visitors alike (Wikipedia, 9Uides).

attractions.name: Blue Mound State Park
attractions.description: Blue Mound State Park is perched atop the highest point in southern Wisconsin, offering spectacular panoramic views of the Driftless Region from its two 40-foot observation towers. The park is renowned for its unique geological features, over 20 miles of scenic trails for hiking and off-road biking, and for hosting the only swimming pool in the Wisconsin State Park System. Wisconsin DNR, Travel Wisconsin

Only swimming pool and splash pad in the Wisconsin State Park System

attractions.name: Breese Stevens Municipal Athletic Field (Wikipedia, Official Site)
attractions.description: Breese Stevens Field is a historic multi-purpose stadium in Madison, Wisconsin, originally constructed in 1925 as a baseball stadium. It is home to the oldest extant masonry grandstand in the state and has been designated a Madison Landmark and a site on the National Register of Historic Places. Today, it serves as a premier outdoor venue for professional soccer, high-profile concerts, and diverse community festivals (Official Site, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Brittingham Park
attractions.description: Brittingham Park is a scenic community park located along the shores of Monona Bay in Madison, offering a mix of active recreation and natural beauty. The park is home to a historic boathouse, an all-inclusive accessible playground, and various athletic facilities, making it a popular destination for outdoor enthusiasts (City of Madison, Madison Mom).

The pub offers a wide selection of Irish whiskeys and properly poured pints of Guinness, alongside a menu that features traditional Irish staples and modern American comfort food. The Kissers, Moda Magazine

attractions.name: Buck & Honey's Restaurant (Monona)
attractions.description: Buck & Honey's is an upscale casual, family-friendly restaurant serving American scratch cuisine in a vibrant and welcoming atmosphere. The Monona location is situated along the Yahara River, offering beautiful waterfront views and a lively setting for dining and entertainment. Established in the spirit of area residents Buck and Honey Birkinbine, the restaurant emphasizes Wisconsin hospitality and community connection.

attractions.name: Cafe Zoma
attractions.description: Cafe Zoma was a beloved, low-key coffee house located in the vibrant Atwood neighborhood of Madison. Known for its no-frills urban interior and extensive selection of coffee drinks, it served as a popular community hub for students and locals alike. The cafe also offered a variety of sweets, sandwiches, and Italian-inspired bakery items before its closure and subsequent rebranding.

attractions.name: Camp Randall Stadium
attractions.description: Camp Randall Stadium is an outdoor collegiate football stadium located on the University of Wisconsin–Madison campus. Opened in 1917, it is the oldest stadium in the Big Ten Conference and serves as the historic home field for the Wisconsin Badgers. The site has significant historical depth, originally serving as a Union Army training camp during the American Civil War. (Wisconsin Badgers), (Wikipedia)
highlights: The famous "Jump Around" tradition where the entire stadium jumps to the House of Pain song between the third and fourth quarters.
The "Fifth Quarter" post-game celebration featuring a 45-minute performance by the University of Wisconsin Marching Band.

attractions.name: Capital Brewery Company, Inc. (Official Website)
attractions.description: Founded in 1984, Capital Brewery is a historic staple of the Madison area, specializing in award-winning traditional German-style lagers. The facility features a massive outdoor Bier Garten known as "Middleton's Backyard" that hosts live music and community events, as well as a cozy indoor Bier Stube for winter months. It is recognized for its commitment to sustainability, utilizing solar power and providing spent grain to local farmers. (Official Website, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Capital City State Trail
attractions.description: The Capital City State Trail is a 17-mile paved recreation trail that serves as a vital link through the Madison metropolitan area, connecting several major regional trails. It traverses scenic landscapes including the Nine Springs E-Way and Capital Springs State Recreation Area, offering views of wetlands, prairies, and lakes while passing through both urban and natural settings Wisconsin DNR, Wikipedia.

attractions.name: Cave of the Mounds - National Natural Landmark
attractions.description: Cave of the Mounds is a premier "show cave" in the Midwest, famous for its stunning array of mineral formations called speleothems. Designated as a National Natural Landmark, it offers guided tours through brightly colored limestone passages and rooms that maintain a constant temperature of 50°F (Cave of the Mounds, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Chazen Museum of Art
attractions.description: The Chazen Museum of Art is a prominent institution on the University of Wisconsin–Madison campus, housing a vast collection of over 25,000 works of art spanning various cultures and historical periods. As the second-largest art museum in Wisconsin, it serves as a center for education, research, and public engagement with the visual arts (Chazen Museum of Art).

attractions.name: Cherokee Marsh Conservation Park
attractions.description: Cherokee Marsh Conservation Park is the largest wetland in Dane County, serving as a vital ecosystem that filters runoff for the Yahara lakes and provides essential habitat for diverse wildlife. The park is managed in three unique units—North, South, and Mendota—spanning over 1,300 acres of restored prairie, oak savanna, woodland, and sedge meadows. City of Madison, Visit Madison
attractions.address: 6098 N. Sherman Ave., 802 Wheeler Rd., and 4338 Beilfuss Dr.

attractions.name: Circus World (Circus World Museum)
attractions.description: Circus World is a 64-acre museum complex and National Historic Landmark located on the original winter quarters of the Ringling Bros. Circus. It preserves the rich heritage of the American circus through extensive exhibits, the world's largest collection of restored circus wagons, and live big-top performances. Wisconsin Historical Society

attractions.description: Colectivo Coffee is a specialty coffee roaster and cafe chain known for its hand-roasted coffee, scratch-made bakery items, and locally sourced food. Their "On the Square" location in Madison is housed in the historic Art Deco Tenney Plaza, offering a unique atmosphere with custom murals and a vibrant community setting near the state capitol.

attractions.name: Comedy on State
attractions.description: Comedy on State is a female-owned and operated premier comedy club located in the heart of downtown Madison, Wisconsin. attractions.description: The Dane County Farmers' Market is the largest producer-only farmers' market in the United States, featuring over 275 vendors throughout the year. attractions.name: Delta Beer Lab (Delta Beer Lab)
attractions.description: Delta Beer Lab is a community-focused brewery and "social laboratory" located in Madison, Wisconsin, that opened in early 2019. Dedicated to its mission of "Crafting Beer, Crafting Change," the business prioritizes quality craft beer while actively supporting local nonprofits and advocating for social equity. The taproom serves as a welcoming destination for all, featuring a variety of scientifically-backed brews and regular community events (Delta Beer Lab).

attractions.name: Devil's Doorway (Devil's Lake State Park)
attractions.description: Devil's Doorway is an iconic quartzite rock formation in Devil's Lake State Park, standing over 31 feet high and forming a natural doorway-shaped arch. It is the park's most photographed feature, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the 360-acre lake and the surrounding Baraboo Hills. Wikipedia), Devil's Lake Visitor Guide

attractions.name: Devil's Lake State Park
attractions.description: Devil's Lake State Park is Wisconsin's most visited state park, featuring spectacular 500-foot-high quartzite bluffs overlooking a 360-acre lake (Wikipedia)). Situated along the Ice Age National Scenic Trail, the park offers a unique geological landscape created by glaciers approximately 12,000 years ago (Wisconsin DNR). It provides extensive recreational opportunities including over 29 miles of hiking trails, world-class rock climbing, and two large sandy beaches (Wisconsin DNR).

Large sandy beaches on the north and south shores for swimming and picnicking (Wisconsin DNR)

attractions.name: Donald County Park
attractions.description: Donald County Park is a 775-acre natural area located southwest of Madison in the Town of Springdale, known for its diverse habitats and rolling topography. The park features unique geological formations like the 110-foot Donald Rock, as well as spring-fed trout streams and restored native prairies. Visitors can enjoy an extensive network of trails for hiking, horseback riding, and snowshoeing, along with picnic areas and historical sites. (Dane County Parks, Friends of Donald Park)

attractions.name: Dotty Dumpling's Dowry
attractions.description: Dotty Dumpling's Dowry is a legendary Madison institution famous for its award-winning gourmet hamburgers and eclectic, trinket-filled atmosphere. Originally established as a gift shop in 1969, it transitioned to a restaurant in 1974 and has since become a staple of the downtown Madison dining scene, known for its unique decor and local craft beer selection. Dotty Dumpling's Dowry, On Wisconsin Magazine

attractions.name: East Washington Avenue Arts Corridor (Capitol East District)

Transformed from a former industrial corridor, the area now hosts world-class rehearsal spaces, contemporary art labs, and major performance stages that serve as a gateway to the city's downtown. attractions.name: Edna Taylor Conservation Park
attractions.description: A 60-acre conservation park featuring a glacial drumlin backbone, diverse marshlands, and restored oak savannas. It is a critical habitat for native species and a significant historical site containing several Native American burial mounds.

attractions.name: Forequarter
attractions.description: Forequarter is an award-winning, intimate neighborhood restaurant and bar in Madison, Wisconsin. Part of the Underground Food Collective, it is celebrated for its inventive, seasonally-driven menu that emphasizes local produce and whole-animal sourcing. The establishment offers a rotating selection of creative small plates and crafted cocktails in a cozy, unpretentious setting.

attractions.name: Glacial Drumlin State Trail
attractions.description: The Glacial Drumlin State Trail is a premier 52-mile recreational rail trail that runs through southern Wisconsin between the Madison and Milwaukee areas. Developed on an abandoned Chicago and North Western Railway corridor, it features a scenic landscape of glacial drumlins, farmlands, marshes, and historic small towns. The trail is primarily surfaced with crushed limestone, with paved asphalt sections near its endpoints in Cottage Grove and Waukesha. Wisconsin DNR
highlights: Spans 52 miles through 10 historic small towns between Cottage Grove and Waukesha Wisconsin DNR
Features unique glacial topography including cigar-shaped hills called drumlins Glacial Drumlin Trail Guide
Crosses the scenic Rock Lake on a long causeway with expansive water views Islands
Traverses diverse natural habitats including the Zeloski Marsh wildlife area Wisconsin Bike Trails
Connects to numerous other regional paths like the Capital City and Fox River trails TrailLink

attractions.name: Gotham New York Bagels & Eats
attractions.description: Gotham New York Bagels & Eats has been a mainstay in the Madison community for over twenty years, serving authentic New York-style bagels made using traditional methods. Every bagel is hand-rolled, kettle-boiled, and baked in a rotating deck oven to achieve the perfect texture. The shop offers a variety of gourmet sandwich creations, house-smoked white fish, and premium smoked salmon, bringing a taste of NYC to the heart of Wisconsin. Gotham Bagels

Gourmet sandwich creations including the popular "Massapequa" chicken sandwich.
House-smoked white fish and premium smoked salmon for traditional deli flavors.

attractions.name: Governor Dodge State Park
attractions.description: Governor Dodge State Park is one of Wisconsin's largest state parks, spanning over 5,000 acres of steep hills, sandstone bluffs, and deep valleys in the scenic Driftless Area. Named after the first governor of the Wisconsin Territory, Henry Dodge, the park features two man-made lakes, a waterfall, and nearly 40 miles of diverse trails for year-round recreation. Wisconsin DNR
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Daily)

Cox Hollow and Twin Valley Lakes for boating and swimming

Winter activities such as cross-country skiing and ice fishing

attractions.name: Governor Nelson State Park
attractions.description: Governor Nelson State Park is a 422-acre day-use park located on the north shore of Lake Mendota, offering scenic views of the Wisconsin State Capitol. Named after former Governor Gaylord Nelson, the park features over 8 miles of trails, a sandy beach, and restored prairie and savanna landscapes. It is a popular destination for boating, fishing, and exploring Native American effigy mounds (Wikipedia, Wisconsin DNR).

Native American effigy mounds located along the Woodland Trail
Large sand beach and swimming area on the shores of Lake Mendota

Restored prairie and oak savanna offering excellent birdwatching opportunities

attractions.name: Graze
attractions.description: Opened in 2010 by James Beard Award-winning Chef Tory Miller, Graze is a lively gastropub offering seasonally inspired comfort food that blends Wisconsin and Korean heritage. The restaurant emphasizes locally sourced ingredients from regional farms and provides a vibrant dining atmosphere with expansive views of the Wisconsin State Capitol.

attractions.name: Great Dane Pub & Brewing Co. (Downtown Madison)
attractions.description: Established in 1994 as Madison's first brewpub, The Great Dane is housed in the historic Fess Hotel building. It is a local institution known for its hand-crafted beers, Wisconsin-inspired pub fare, and a warm atmosphere featuring multiple bars and a beautiful outdoor garden. For over 30 years, it has been a cornerstone of the downtown Madison dining and social scene.

attractions.name: Harvest Restaurant
attractions.description: Harvest Restaurant is a renowned farm-to-table dining establishment in downtown Madison, featuring a menu enhanced by locally-grown, organic ingredients. The offerings range from fresh fish to naturally-raised beef and lamb, all crafted with seasonal bounty to reflect the flavors of Wisconsin (Travel Wisconsin, Nova Circle).

attractions.name: Henry Vilas Zoo Official Site
attractions.description: Henry Vilas Zoo is a 28-acre public zoo in Madison, Wisconsin, that is accredited by the Association of Zoos and Aquariums (AZA). Founded in 1911, it is one of only 10 remaining free admission zoos in North America and receives over 750,000 visitors annually. Official Site, Wikipedia

attractions.name: High Noon Saloon
attractions.description: High Noon Saloon is a premier live music venue in Madison's Capitol East District, founded in 2004 to carry on the legacy of the city's legendary rock clubs (High Noon Saloon). The venue hosts an eclectic mix of national and local acts across genres like rock, indie, folk, and hip hop in a rustic space featuring two bars and an elevated stage (Visit Madison). It is well-regarded for its welcoming atmosphere, professional sound system, and popular seasonal outdoor patio series (The Knot).

attractions.name: Hmong Legacy Market
attractions.description: Hmong Legacy Market is a family-owned grocery store and deli that has served as a cultural cornerstone for Madison’s Hmong community since 2016. The market features a wide variety of Southeast Asian staples and a popular hot deli known for authentic dishes like Hmong sausage, crispy pork belly, and papaya salad. The park features separate enclosures for large and small breeds, providing a safe environment for socialization and exercise amidst a grassy field with shaded areas. Its nearly 300-acre headquarters features the only place in the world where visitors can see all 15 crane species, alongside interactive exhibits and restored natural habitats (Wikipedia, Official Site).

attractions.description: Located in downtown Madison, James Madison Park is a 12.63-acre waterfront community park on the shores of Lake Mendota. It offers a blend of active recreation and historical landmarks, providing a peaceful escape for students and residents alike with its open grassy areas and scenic lake views (City of Madison, Wikipedia).

Now led by James Beard Award-winning Chef Tory Miller, the restaurant offers a refined culinary experience featuring seasonally inspired menus crafted from the finest local ingredients provided by Wisconsin farmers and artisan producers (L'Etoile History).

attractions.name: L.R. Ingersoll Wonders of Physics Museum
attractions.description: The L.R. Ingersoll Wonders of Physics Museum is a hands-on, kid-friendly educational venue featuring over 65 interactive exhibits that bring physical concepts to life. Established in 1918 and located on the UW-Madison campus, it offers self-paced exploration of topics ranging from mechanics and fluid dynamics to modern physics and chaos theory. (Official Website, Atlas Obscura)

Plasma Tube - A visual display of ionized gas for modern physics exploration.
Lissajous Curve Demonstration - Visualizing sound and frequency through geometry.

attractions.name: Lake Mendota

It defines the northern shore of the Madison isthmus and provides a scenic backdrop for the University of Wisconsin-Madison campus. Clean Lakes Alliance, Lakeshore Nature Preserve, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Madison Isthmus (Downtown Madison)
attractions.description: The Madison Isthmus is the narrow strip of land between Lake Mendota and Lake Monona that forms the heart of downtown Madison, Wisconsin. It is home to the Wisconsin State Capitol, the University of Wisconsin–Madison, and vibrant pedestrian corridors like State Street. This unique geographic feature defines the city's "Lake, City, Lake" character and serves as a hub for government, education, and culture.

Dane County Farmers' Market: The nation's largest producer-only farmers' market, held on Capitol Square.

Housed in a striking glass facility designed by world-renowned architect César Pelli, the museum serves as a vital cultural anchor in the heart of downtown Madison. Its diverse offerings range from a permanent collection of nearly 6,000 objects to innovative new-media installations and a scenic rooftop sculpture garden. (MMoCA), (Visit Downtown Madison)

Popular late-night menu available Wednesday through Saturday starting at 10 PM (Official Website)
Located in a beautifully restored historic building from the late 1800s (Lauer Realty Group)
Traditional table service including \\"vertical\\" relish trays and fresh-baked bread (Tripadvisor)
Extensive wine list and expertly crafted classic cocktails, including the Brandy Old Fashioned (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Tyrol Basin Ski and Snowboard Area
attractions.description: Tyrol Basin is a versatile year-round outdoor recreation area nestled in the picturesque Driftless Area, just west of Madison, Wisconsin. Best known for its premier ski and snowboard slopes and top-ranked terrain parks, the facility also offers snow tubing, disc golf, and a seasonal haunted attraction. The site features a historic barn-turned-lodge housing the Tyrol Tavern and Restaurant, providing a cozy atmosphere for dining and live music.
attractions.days_open: Daily (Winter Season); Wed, Thu, Fri (Summer - Tavern); Daily (Summer - Disc Golf)
highlights: 17-22 ski and snowboard runs ranging from beginner to double black diamond expert levels Tripadvisor
Award-winning terrain parks frequently voted among the best in the Midwest Wisconsin Trail Guide
Championship 18-hole disc golf course spanning 40 acres of steep slopes and deep valleys Tyrol Basin Summer Activities
Tyrol Tavern and Restaurant located in a historic barn, offering a classic Wisconsin fish fry and live music Tyrol Basin Tavern
'Terror at Tyrol' seasonal haunted house featuring 5 terrifying experiences in October Terror at Tyrol
Scenic chairlift rides available during peak fall foliage weekends in September and October Tyrol Basin Summer Activities

attractions.name: Union South
attractions.description: Known as the "south campus living room," Union South is a LEED Gold-certified social hub for the University of Wisconsin–Madison community. The 2011 building features a 350-seat movie theater, a multi-level recreation center, eight dining options, and an eco-conscious boutique hotel.
highlights: The Sett: A three-level entertainment center featuring bowling, billiards, and an indoor rock climbing wall.
The Marquee Cinema: A state-of-the-art 326-seat theater hosting free film screenings and university lectures.
Badger Bash: A popular gameday celebration held 2.5 hours before every home football game with live music and band performances.
LEED Gold Sustainability: A landmark for eco-friendly design, featuring sustainable materials and energy-efficient technologies.
Diverse Dining: Offers local favorites like Babcock Dairy ice cream at The Daily Scoop and pan-Asian cuisine at Ginger Root.
Wisconsin Union Hotel: A unique on-site hotel providing a central stay for visitors and alumni steps from Camp Randall.

attractions.name: University of Wisconsin Geology Museum
attractions.description: The University of Wisconsin Geology Museum is a science and research institution that explores Wisconsin's deep history through a vast collection of rocks, minerals, and fossils. Visitors can view dinosaur skeletons, touch ancient rocks, and explore exhibits on meteorites and glowing minerals in its 3,000-square-foot facility. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday (Official Website)
highlights: Boaz Mastodon skeleton (Official Website)
Triceratops skull and dinosaur gallery (Official Website)
Fluorescent minerals that glow in the dark (Official Website)
Walk-through model of a limestone cave (Tripadvisor)
Meteorite and astrobiology collection (Official Website)
Silurian soft-bodied fauna fossils (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: University of Wisconsin–Madison
attractions.description: Since its founding in 1848, the University of Wisconsin–Madison has been a catalyst for the extraordinary. As a public land-grant university and major research institution, it is situated on 939 acres along the scenic shore of Lake Mendota. The university is guided by the 'Wisconsin Idea,' the principle that education should influence and improve people’s lives beyond the boundaries of the classroom. (UW-Madison)
attractions.address: 1308 W. Dayton Street (Union South Welcome Desk)
highlights: Bascom Hill: The historic heart of campus with iconic views and the Abraham Lincoln statue. (Wikipedia)
Memorial Union Terrace: A famous lakeside destination known for its sunburst chairs and Babcock ice cream. (Wisconsin Union)
Allen Centennial Garden: A beautiful outdoor living laboratory and public botanical garden. (Allen Centennial Garden)
Chazen Museum of Art: A prominent campus museum featuring diverse permanent collections and traveling exhibits. (Chazen Museum)
Babcock Hall Dairy Store: Home to world-famous ice cream and award-winning cheeses made on-site. (Babcock Dairy)
Camp Randall Stadium: The historic home of the Wisconsin Badgers football team. (Yelp)

attractions.name: University of Wisconsin–Madison Arboretum (Official Website)
attractions.description: The University of Wisconsin–Madison Arboretum is a 1,200-acre National Historic Landmark widely recognized as the birthplace of ecological restoration. It features the oldest and most varied collection of restored ecological communities in the world, including tallgrass prairies, savannas, wetlands, and several forest types. Visitors can explore over 17 miles of trails and world-class horticultural collections designed for research, education, and recreation. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
highlights: Curtis Prairie, the world's oldest restored prairie and a pioneer site for ecological restoration research (Official Website)
Longenecker Horticultural Gardens, featuring Wisconsin's largest and most diverse collection of woody plants with over 2,600 taxa (Official Website)
The Wisconsin Native Plant Garden, showcasing hundreds of native species in 15 themed gardens surrounding the Visitor Center (Official Website)
Over 17 miles of trails through diverse ecosystems including the Leopold Pines and Wingra Woods (Official Website)
Ancient effigy mounds located within the protected woodland areas of the grounds (Official Website)

attractions.name: University of Wisconsin–Madison Lakeshore Nature Preserve
attractions.description: The University of Wisconsin–Madison Lakeshore Nature Preserve is a 300-acre natural area situated on the south shore of Lake Mendota. It serves as a living classroom for the university's teaching and research needs while providing a place for respite and well-being for the campus and community. The preserve includes 4.3 miles of shoreline, featuring diverse environments such as prairies, wetlands, and deep woods. Lakeshore Nature Preserve, Wikipedia
highlights: Picnic Point - A famous nearly mile-long peninsula with iconic views of the Madison skyline.
Howard Temin Lakeshore Path - A scenic 4.3-mile trail popular with walkers, joggers, and bicyclists. Source
Native American Effigy Mounds - Ancient burial mounds including the Eagle Heights and Picnic Point groups. Source
Biocore Prairie - A key site for ecological research and restoration efforts. Source
Class of 1918 Marsh - A restored wetland providing excellent birdwatching opportunities. Source
Frautschi Point - A quiet wooded area offering spectacular lake views and birding. Source

attractions.name: Vilas (Henry) Park
attractions.description: Vilas (Henry) Park is one of Madison's oldest and most popular parks, curving along the scenic northern shore of Lake Wingra. The park offers year-round recreation, featuring a sandy swimming beach, fishing piers, and a lagoon that hosts ice skating and hockey in the winter. It is uniquely integrated with the adjacent Henry Vilas Zoo and the University of Wisconsin–Madison Arboretum. (City of Madison, Visit Madison-park/183133/))
highlights: Henry Vilas Zoo (adjacent and admission-free)
Vilas Beach on the shores of Lake Wingra
Historic 'Old Woman in a Shoe' playground slide
Native American burial mounds (National Register of Historic Places)
Winter ice skating and hockey on the park lagoon
Accessible fishing piers and boat launch for Lake Wingra

attractions.name: Vintage Brewing Company (Madison West)
attractions.description: Vintage Brewing Company is a family-owned and operated brewpub known for its "elevated Americana" cuisine and an eclectic array of highly-acclaimed house-made brews. The establishment offers a relaxed, retro ambiance with Midwestern hospitality, serving as both a local neighborhood haunt and a destination for craft beer enthusiasts. Vintage Brewing Co.
highlights: Extensive selection of house-made craft beers, including specialty and barrel-aged options Vintage Brewing Co.
"Elevated Americana" menu featuring classic pub fare, burgers, and fish baskets Tripadvisor
Daily Happy Hour specials on vintage taps, rail cocktails, and appetizers Vintage Brewing Co.
Sunday Brunch service with a dedicated menu Vintage Brewing Co.
Outdoor patio seating available during warmer months Vintage Brewing Co.
Loyalty club offering rewards and birthday bonuses Vintage Brewing Co.

attractions.name: Warner Park
attractions.description: Distinctive for its natural beauty and expansive tracts of land, Warner Park is a major municipal park on Madison's northeast side. It serves as a hub for community festivals and sporting events, most notably as the home of the Madison Mallards baseball team at 'The Duck Pond' stadium. City of Madison, Wikipedia
highlights: Warner Stadium, nicknamed "The Duck Pond," home to the Madison Mallards baseball team.
The Warner Park Community Recreation Center, a facility for fitness, classes, and community events.
Frautschi Family Accessible Playground, featuring inclusive equipment and a rubberized surface.
Large 8.5-acre off-leash dog park with fenced areas.
Warner Park Lagoon, popular for fishing and winter ice skating.
Public boat launch providing direct access to Lake Mendota.

attractions.name: Weary Traveler Freehouse
attractions.description: The Weary Traveler Freehouse is a cozy neighborhood joint on Madison's near east side, housed in a historic former grocery store. It features a hodgepodge vintage setting with board games, eclectic decor, and international comfort food ranging from Hungarian Goulash to the famous Bob's Bad Breath Burger.
highlights: Bob's Bad Breath Burger with cream cheese and green goddess dressing
Cozy vintage atmosphere with eclectic decor and board games
Live music on Thursday and Sunday nights
International comfort food menu including Hungarian Goulash and Tom Ka Tofu
Magic Hour (Happy Hour) specials everyday from 3pm-6pm and 10pm-Close
Locally sourced ingredients and elevated pub fare

attractions.name: Westmorland Park Ice Rink
attractions.description: Westmorland Park features a popular seasonal outdoor ice skating facility located in the heart of Madison's near west side. The site offers both a general skating rink and a dedicated hockey rink with boards, nets, and lighting for evening use. It is a community-focused destination maintained through a partnership between the city and neighborhood volunteers. (City of Madison, Mad City Dream Homes)
attractions.days_open: Daily during the winter season (typically mid-December to February, weather permitting) (City of Madison)
highlights: Features two separate rinks: one for general ice skating and one for hockey with boards and lights (City of Madison)
Offers free loaner skates in various sizes at the warming shelter on a first-come, first-served basis (City of Madison)
Maintained by the neighborhood's 'Adopt Ice' volunteers to ensure high-quality skating conditions (City of Madison)
Includes a seasonal warming shelter with restrooms that are open during rink hours (Travel Wisconsin)
Equipped with overhead lighting for night skating sessions (Mad City Dream Homes)
Located in a scenic park that also features a small sledding hill for additional winter fun (Mad City Dream Homes)

attractions.name: Willy Street (Williamson-Marquette Neighborhood)
attractions.description: Affectionately known as "Willy Street," this eclectic and bohemian district is the heart of Madison's Marquette neighborhood. It is renowned for its historic 19th-century architecture, vibrant murals, and a high concentration of independent, locally owned shops and restaurants. The area serves as a hub for eco-conscious living and hosts several of the city's most popular community festivals. Destination Madison, Islands
attractions.phone: (608) 251-6776 (Willy Street Co-op East) Willy Street Co-op
highlights: Willy Street Co-op: A landmark natural foods cooperative and community cornerstone. Willy Street Co-op
Machinery Row: A historic brick commercial block on the National Register of Historic Places. Wikipedia
Annual Festivals: Home to major events like the Willy Street Fair, Orton Park Festival, and Marquette Waterfront Festival. Marquette Neighborhood Association
Eclectic Dining: A foodie destination featuring diverse cuisines from Laotian to Pakistani and traditional pub fare. Destination Madison
Orton Park: Madison's first public park, featuring ancient oaks and historic monuments. City of Madison
Independent Boutiques: A wide array of thrift stores, fair-trade shops, and local artisan galleries. Traverse Blog

attractions.name: Wingra Park and Boat Livery
attractions.description: Wingra Park is a scenic 11.76-acre neighborhood park situated on the northern shoreline of Lake Wingra in Madison, Wisconsin. It is highly popular for its watercraft rentals, fishing opportunities, and family-friendly amenities such as a playground and basketball court. The park serves as a hub for outdoor recreation in the Dudgeon-Monroe neighborhood, offering beautiful views and access to the lake's calm, no-wake waters. (City of Madison, Wikipedia)
highlights: Watercraft rentals including canoes, kayaks, and paddle boats through Madison Boats (City of Madison)
Public boat launch for access to the no-wake Lake Wingra (City of Madison)
Recreational facilities including a basketball court and playground (City of Madison)
Multipurpose athletic field used for soccer and community events (City of Madison)
Popular fishing spots and shoreline access (City of Madison)
Picnic areas overlooking the lake (City of Madison)

attractions.name: Wisconsin Brewing Company (Lake Louie Brewing)
attractions.description: Wisconsin Brewing Company, now integrated with Lake Louie Brewing, is a family-owned brewery committed to crafting memorable and drinkable beers. Located just minutes from Madison, the facility features a spacious taproom and a backyard patio with views of a 9-acre pond. (Lake Louie Brewing, Tripadvisor)
highlights: Spacious backyard patio overlooking a 9-acre pond (Tripadvisor)
Offers a selection of 12-14 craft beers on tap (Lake Louie Brewing)
Live music and free outdoor concerts during summer months (Tripadvisor)
Family-friendly and dog-friendly environment (Lake Louie Brewing)
Carry-in food and local delivery options are welcomed (Lake Louie Brewing)
Joint operation of Wisconsin Brewing Company and Lake Louie Brewing (Lake Louie Brewing)

attractions.name: Wisconsin Dells
attractions.description: Wisconsin Dells is a vibrant Midwestern city and premier vacation destination famously known as the "Waterpark Capital of the World." Located along the scenic Wisconsin River, it features a unique combination of natural sandstone formations, world-class indoor and outdoor waterparks, and diverse family entertainment. Visitors can enjoy everything from thrilling waterslides and theme parks to tranquil boat tours and charming downtown shopping Wikipedia, Official Website.
highlights: World-class indoor and outdoor waterparks, including Noah's Ark and Kalahari Resorts Official Site
Scenic boat tours showcasing the unique sandstone formations of the Wisconsin River Travel Wisconsin
Thrilling theme parks and wooden roller coasters at Mt. Olympus Water & Theme Park YouTube
Interactive family adventures and specialty museums like Wizard Quest and Tommy Bartlett Exploratory Tripadvisor
The enchanting natural beauty of Witches Gulch and the Scenic River Walk Official Site
A charming downtown district filled with classic shops, dining, and live entertainment City of Wisconsin Dells

attractions.name: Wisconsin Historical Museum
attractions.description: The Wisconsin Historical Museum is a state history museum currently operating out of the History Maker Space while a new, world-class history center is constructed. It offers a variety of pop-up exhibits, educational programs, and walking tours that explore Wisconsin's rich cultural heritage and historical milestones. Wisconsin Historical Museum
attractions.days_open: Open for special events, experiences, and programs; Field trips by appointment only. Wisconsin Historical Museum
highlights: History Maker Space: A temporary programming hub on Capitol Square featuring pop-up exhibits and sneak peeks of the future museum. Wisconsin Historical Museum
Madison Black History Walking Tours: Expert-led explorations of the downtown streets and neighborhoods shaped by Madison's early Black communities. Wisconsin Historical Museum
Madison’s LGBTQ+ History Walking Tour: A guided tour highlighting the places and spaces that established Madison as a hub for LGBTQ+ rights. Wisconsin Historical Museum
Road America Exhibition: An upcoming specialty exhibition at the new center focusing on the history of Wisconsin’s iconic national park of speed. Wisconsin Historical Museum
Education Programs: Hands-on history field trips and summer camps designed for all ages to learn about Wisconsin's past. Wisconsin Historical Museum
Wisconsin Historical Museum Store: A retail shop featuring unique items, books, and souvenirs that support the Society's mission. Wisconsin Historical Museum

attractions.name: Wisconsin State Capitol
attractions.description: Constructed between 1906 and 1917, the Wisconsin State Capitol houses the state's legislative, executive, and judicial branches. It features four equal wings and a central white granite dome that reaches a height of 284.4 feet, making it the tallest building in Madison. Wisconsin Department of Administration, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed on New Year's Day, Easter, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, and Christmas) Wisconsin.gov
highlights: Central Rotunda and Dome reaching 284.4 feet
Ornate chambers for the State Senate and Assembly
The Wisconsin Supreme Court room with murals and velvet finishes
The Governor's Conference Room featuring historic artwork
Sixth-floor museum and outdoor observation deck (open seasonally)
Statues and sculptures including 'Wisconsin' atop the dome

attractions.name: Wisconsin Union Theater
attractions.description: The Wisconsin Union Theater is a premier performing arts center located within the University of Wisconsin–Madison's Memorial Union. It serves as both a world-class venue for international artists and a hands-on learning laboratory for student leaders involved in artistic curation and event production Official Website.
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Box Office), Daily (Building) Official Website
highlights: Shannon Hall: A large, historic performance space with seating for over 1,100 people and superb acoustics Wikipedia
Play Circle Theater: An intimate black box performance space with flexible seating for up to 182 guests Official Website
Student Leadership: Managed in part by the Wisconsin Union Directorate, providing students with hands-on experience in the arts Official Website
Diverse Programming: Hosts a wide range of events including jazz, classical music, dance, world stage performances, and lectures Wikipedia
Historic Legacy: Opened in 1939, it has hosted legendary figures like Martin Luther King Jr., Ella Fitzgerald, and John F. Kennedy Wikipedia

attractions.name: Wisconsin Veterans Museum
attractions.description: The Wisconsin Veterans Museum is an award-winning educational activity of the Wisconsin Department of Veterans Affairs dedicated to sharing and preserving the stories of Wisconsin's military veterans. Located in the heart of Madison, the museum features two main galleries that chronicle the history-shaping exploits of Wisconsinites from the Civil War to the present day (Wisconsin Veterans Museum, Travel Wisconsin).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday through Saturday year-round; Sundays from April through September (Wisconsin Veterans Museum)
highlights: 19th Century Gallery showcasing Wisconsin's involvement in the Civil War, Spanish-American War, and the Philippine War (Wikipedia)
Life-sized Antietam Diorama depicting a pivotal Civil War battle (Wisconsin Veterans Museum)
Old Abe the war eagle, the famous mascot of the 8th Wisconsin Infantry Regiment (Wisconsin Veterans Museum)
20th Century to Present Gallery featuring exhibits on World War I, World War II, Korea, Vietnam, and 21st-century conflicts (Wisconsin Veterans Museum)
Three full-scale aircraft: a WWI Sopwith Camel, a WWII P-51 Mustang, and a Vietnam-era Huey helicopter (Wikipedia)
Accredited Smithsonian Affiliate status since 2014 (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Working Draft Beer Company (Working Draft Beer Company)
attractions.description: Working Draft Beer Company is a communal craft brewery and taproom founded in 2018 that focuses on producing world-class European lagers and hoppy ales. The space is designed to be a welcoming, art-filled environment where people can gather over fresh, hand-crafted beer brewed on-site (Working Draft Beer Company).
attractions.address: 1129 East Wilson Street, Madison, WI 53703 (Working Draft Beer Company)
highlights: European Lagers & Hoppy Ales brewed on-site (Working Draft Beer Company)
Artist-in-Residence program supporting local creators (Working Draft Beer Company)
Communal taproom with bars made from salvaged bowling alley lanes (Working Draft Beer Company)
Regular community events including Yoga on Tap and Samba Night (Working Draft Beer Company)
Dog-friendly taproom with an open, welcoming atmosphere (Working Draft Beer Company)
Rotating selection of local food trucks and pop-ups (Working Draft Beer Company)

attractions.name: World's Largest Brat Fest
attractions.description: The World’s Largest Brat Fest is a massive Memorial Day weekend fundraiser and community celebration featuring over 100 free musical acts, a carnival, and millions of brats served for charity. Originally founded in 1983 as a customer appreciation event at Metcalfe’s Hilldale location, it has evolved into a major Madison tradition that has raised over $2.4 million for local non-profits. Brat Fest History
attractions.days_open: Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Memorial Day Weekend) Alliant Energy Center
highlights: World's Largest Brat Fest fundraiser supporting over 100 local charities
Over 100 free musical acts performing on multiple stages throughout the weekend
Take Your Brat to Work Day: A Friday morning drive-thru tradition starting at 6 AM
Mr. Ed’s Magical Midways Carnival with rides and games for all ages
Interactive zones including the Kid Zone, Sports Zone, and a Farm Zone with petting animals
"Ride Your Bike" program offering a free brat to those who cycle to the fest on Friday morning

attractions.name: Yahara Place Park
attractions.description: Yahara Place Park is a peaceful 6-acre neighborhood park located along the shores of Lake Monona and the Yahara River. It offers a variety of recreational amenities including a playground, sports courts, and scenic open spaces for picnics and lakeside relaxation (City of Madison, Badgers Branch Out).
highlights: Playground for children
Basketball and volleyball courts
Horseshoe pits and multipurpose fields
Shoreline access for fishing on Lake Monona and the Yahara River
Sid Boyum "Fu Dog Lantern" sculpture
Scenic walking trails and benches for lake viewing
